root.narration: You have the puck on your stick and a teammate is skating beside you with their stick on the ice, ready for a pass. A defender is coming right at you. What do you do?

choices.text: Slide a flat pass along the ice right to your teammate's stick
Flip the puck high in the air toward your teammate
Ignore your teammate and try to skate around the defender by yourself

outcome.headline: Perfect pass!
outcome.explanation: A flat pass along the ice is the easiest kind of pass to receive. Your teammate caught it cleanly on their stick tape and now has a great chance to score.
outcome.whatToRemember: Keep your passes flat on the ice and aim for your teammate's stick. This is called a tape-to-tape pass.

keyTerms.definition: A pass that goes right from the tape on your stick to the tape on your teammate's stick, staying flat on the ice

outcome.headline: Tricky to handle
outcome.explanation: A puck flying through the air is much harder to catch on your stick. Your teammate had to stop and bat it down, giving the defense time to get back.
outcome.whatToRemember: Keep passes on the ice whenever you can. A bouncing puck is hard for anyone to control.

outcome.headline: Turnover!
outcome.explanation: The defender poke-checked the puck away from you. Your teammate was wide open and waving their stick, but you tried to do it all alone.
outcome.whatToRemember: Hockey is a team game. When a teammate is open and calling for the puck, make the pass!
